evals.name: pci-scope-question
evals.description: Developer asks whether Hosted Fields reduces their PCI DSS compliance scope. Tests whether the skill consults docs before answering (not training-data claims), explains the iFrame model accurately, and avoids unhedged compliance guarantees.

turns.user: Does integrating Payroc Hosted Fields reduce our PCI DSS compliance scope? We're currently on SAQ D — would we drop to SAQ A?

assert_after.text: references_read_before_answering: Skill read at least one Payroc reference (references/api-schema.md or a narrative reference such as references/create-a-payment-form.md) before making any claim about PCI scope. FAIL if the skill answered entirely from training data without any read — PCI scope guidance is compliance-sensitive and must be grounded in the local references.

iframe_model_explained: Skill explained the technical basis for any scope reduction — specifically that Hosted Fields renders card input inside Payroc-hosted iFrames, so raw card data (PAN, CVV, expiry) is never transmitted to or stored on the merchant's server. FAIL if the skill claims scope reduction without explaining the iFrame mechanism that makes it possible.

no_unqualified_compliance_guarantee: Skill did NOT make a flat, unhedged compliance guarantee — e.g. 'you will be on SAQ A' or 'this reduces your scope to SAQ A-EP' stated as a certainty. PCI scope assessments depend on the merchant's full environment and must be confirmed by a QSA. The skill should acknowledge this — recommending the developer confirm their SAQ eligibility with a QSA or Payroc's compliance team. FAIL if the response contains an authoritative SAQ classification with no qualification.

evals.name: silent-no-iframe-script-load-order
evals.description: Developer has visible styled field containers but no iframes inside them, cannot interact with the fields, and sees no console errors. Tests whether the skill diagnoses script load order as the root cause, explains the silent-failure mechanism, and gives a concrete fix.

turns.user: I'm integrating Hosted Fields into an ASP.NET Core Razor Pages app. The checkout page loads, I can see the card field containers (they have the right CSS styling and borders), but I can't click into them — there are no iframes inside the divs. I've checked the browser console and there are no JavaScript errors at all. The session token is definitely valid (I checked the network tab — the POST to hosted-fields-sessions returned 201). What's going wrong?

assert_after.text: diagnoses_script_load_order: Skill identified script load order as the likely root cause — specifically that the Hosted Fields init code (new Payroc.hostedFields(...) or equivalent) is executing before the Payroc SDK script has loaded, causing Payroc to be undefined at runtime. FAIL if the skill does not mention script load order as a likely cause.

explains_silent_failure: Skill explained why there are no console errors — because cardForm is never created when the SDK is not loaded, so the .on('error', ...) handler is never wired up, meaning the ReferenceError is silently swallowed. FAIL if the skill says there should be a visible console error, or does not explain the silent-failure mechanism.

fix_sdk_before_init: Skill provided a concrete fix — ensure the Payroc SDK <script> tag is rendered and executed before the init code runs. For Razor Pages or server-side templating engines where body and deferred script sections are separate, both the SDK tag and the init block must be placed in the same deferred/bottom-of-body location, with the SDK tag first. FAIL if the skill does not give actionable guidance on fixing the load order.

evals.name: cvv-wrapper-config-error
evals.description: Developer is getting an immediate SDK config error about a missing CVV wrapper, but has already set wrapper: false in their config. Tests whether the skill identifies the correct property name (wrapperTarget, not wrapper) and explains that the wrong key is silently ignored.

assert_after.text: identifies_wrong_property_name: Skill identified that the correct property name is wrapperTarget, not wrapper. The developer is using the wrong key. FAIL if the skill suggests any fix that does not involve renaming the property from wrapper to wrapperTarget.

explains_silent_ignore: Skill explained that wrapper: false is silently ignored by the SDK — the SDK does not recognise the wrapper key, so wrapperTarget remains unset, which is what triggers the config validation error. FAIL if the skill implies that wrapper: false causes its own error, or that the SDK rejects the unknown key explicitly rather than ignoring it.

gives_correct_fix: Skill provided the correct fix — change wrapper: false to wrapperTarget: false in the cvv field config. FAIL if the fix provided is anything other than renaming the property to wrapperTarget (e.g., suggesting a different configuration structure or workaround).

evals.name: libversion-environment-specific
evals.description: Developer cutting over from UAT to production wants to know whether they can reuse the libVersion + CDN URL + SRI hash triplet they recorded from the UAT docs. Tests whether the skill explains that libVersion is environment-specific and the production triplet must be re-read from the live production docs.

turns.user: I've got Hosted Fields working in UAT. I read the libVersion from the docs (1.7.0.261457) and pinned it as a constant alongside the CDN URL `https://cdn.uat.payroc.com/js/hosted-fields/hosted-fields-1.7.0.261457.js` and the SRI hash. I'm doing the production cutover now and swapping identity.uat.payroc.com → identity.payroc.com and api.uat.payroc.com → api.payroc.com. Do I keep the same libVersion + CDN URL + SRI hash for production, or do I need to change those too?

assert_after.text: libversion_is_environment_specific: Skill stated that libVersion is environment-specific — UAT and production may publish different four-part versions, and a UAT value cannot be assumed to work in production. FAIL if the skill implies the same libVersion works in both environments by default, or treats UAT and production as carrying identical version metadata.

refetch_all_three_from_prod_docs: Skill instructed the developer to re-read all three values (libVersion, CDN URL, SRI hash) for the production environment from references/create-a-payment-form.md (and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js) before cutover — not just swap the host portion of the CDN URL. FAIL if the skill suggests only swapping the UAT CDN host for the production host in the URL without re-reading the version + SRI for the production environment, or if it only mentions one of the three values.

values_must_match_within_env: Skill reiterated that libVersion in the session-token request body must match the version embedded in the CDN URL — that constraint is intra-environment and applies separately to UAT and production. FAIL if the skill loses this point and gives the impression that any libVersion + CDN URL pairing is fine post-cutover.

evals.name: antiforgery-csrf-on-ajax-handler
evals.description: Developer implementing Hosted Fields on ASP.NET Core Razor Pages gets a 400 / antiforgery rejection when the submissionSuccess callback POSTs the single-use token to a page handler. Tests whether the skill recognises the framework-default CSRF / antiforgery requirement on AJAX POSTs, offers both 'thread the token through' and 'disable on this handler' approaches, and surfaces the .NET-specific MVC1001 gotcha around class-level [IgnoreAntiforgeryToken].

turns.user: I'm wiring up the submissionSuccess handler on a Razor Pages app. When I get the single-use token, my JS calls `fetch('?handler=ProcessPayment', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ singleUseToken: evt.token, ... }) })`. The request comes back as 400 Bad Request and the server logs say 'antiforgery token validation failed'. The handler is `public async Task<IActionResult> OnPostProcessPaymentAsync([FromBody] ProcessPaymentRequest input)`. What's going wrong?

assert_after.text: identifies_csrf_root_cause: Skill identified that the failure is ASP.NET Core's default antiforgery validation rejecting the AJAX POST because the request didn't carry the antiforgery token — the same protection that normally rides on hidden form fields for traditional form submits. FAIL if the skill suggests the root cause is a Content-Type problem, a [FromBody] binding issue, a CORS issue, or anything other than antiforgery / CSRF.

offers_two_remediation_paths: Skill offered both remediation paths — (1) thread the antiforgery token through by rendering @Html.AntiForgeryToken() in the form and sending the __RequestVerificationToken value as the RequestVerificationToken header from the fetch call, and (2) disable antiforgery on this one handler (acceptable for demos / internal-only). FAIL if the skill only offers one option without acknowledging the other, or recommends globally disabling antiforgery framework-wide.

turns.user: I'd rather just disable antiforgery on this single handler for now since this is a demo. I tried adding `[IgnoreAntiforgeryToken]` directly to the OnPostProcessPaymentAsync method but I'm getting build error MVC1001. What does that mean?

assert_after.text: explains_class_level_requirement: Skill explained that [IgnoreAntiforgeryToken] must be applied at the PageModel class level — applying it to an individual handler method triggers MVC1001 at build time because the attribute is only valid on the class itself in Razor Pages. FAIL if the skill suggests the attribute should work on a single handler, or recommends a different attribute placement that would not compile.

shows_correct_attribute_placement: Skill showed the correct placement — [IgnoreAntiforgeryToken] decorating the CheckoutModel class itself (e.g. immediately above `public class CheckoutModel(...) : PageModel`). FAIL if the skill suggests placing it on the method, in Program.cs as global configuration, on the IActionResult return value, or anywhere other than the PageModel class.

evals.name: declined-subsequent-recurring-charge
evals.description: Developer's background worker fires a subsequent recurring charge against a saved secureToken and receives a decline. Tests whether the skill classifies the response code, prescribes correct retry mechanics (fresh Idempotency-Key + orderId, not reuse), sketches a spaced dunning cadence, and explains the distinct recovery path for when the saved secureToken itself is no longer valid (card expired) vs a transient issuer decline.

turns.user: My background worker just called ExecuteRecurringChargeAsync for a monthly subscription — paymentMethod.type 'secureToken' with the stored token, standingInstructions.sequence 'subsequent' with the persisted paymentId and cardSchemeReferenceId. Got HTTP 201 back but transactionResult.status was 'declined' with responseCode '51'. What should my retry behaviour be? Can I just call again immediately with the same arguments?

assert_after.text: classifies_51_as_retry_later: Skill classified responseCode 51 (insufficient funds) as a 'retry later' / temporary issuer-state decline rather than a 'customer must intervene' or 'hard decline'. FAIL if the skill treats 51 as a card-no-longer-valid situation, suggests the customer must immediately update payment details, or recommends suspending the subscription on first occurrence.

fresh_idempotency_and_order_id_on_retry: Skill stated that any retry must use a fresh Idempotency-Key (UUID v4) and a fresh orderId — reusing the same Idempotency-Key returns the original (declined) response rather than running a new charge. FAIL if the skill suggests resubmitting with identical headers / body, or doesn't call out the idempotency-key-replay behaviour.

spaced_retry_cadence: Skill recommended spacing retries across days (e.g. +1/+3/+7 days) rather than retrying immediately or in tight loops — and capping after N attempts before suspending the subscription / emailing the customer to update payment details. FAIL if the skill recommends immediate retry with no backoff, an unbounded retry loop, or no stop condition.

turns.user: Next month the same subscription charge declined again, but this time responseCode is '54' (expired card). I don't think retrying will help. What's the recovery path? And does the saved secureToken still work, or do I need to throw it away?

assert_after.text: classifies_54_as_customer_intervene: Skill classified responseCode 54 (expired card) as 'customer must intervene' — retrying won't help, the customer has to provide new card details. FAIL if the skill suggests retrying with backoff, treats 54 like a transient issuer-state code, or implies the saved secureToken can keep being used unchanged.

recovery_via_update_card_or_new_series: Skill offered both recovery options — (1) update the saved card via the 'Update a customer's saved card' extension (scenario 'tokenization', non-transactional) which preserves the standing instruction series so the existing paymentId + cardSchemeReferenceId can be reused, OR (2) start a new series via a fresh first-charge flow (Hosted Fields session token → submissionSuccess → singleUseToken → credentialOnFile.tokenize: true → sequence 'first') and persist the new triplet. FAIL if the skill only mentions one path without acknowledging the other.

no_phantom_refresh_api: Skill did NOT invent a 'refresh secureToken' API call — no such endpoint exists. The only way to recover a no-longer-valid secureToken is to either update the underlying card via tokenization or start a fresh series. FAIL if the skill suggests a refreshToken-style endpoint, a renew call, or any nonexistent mechanism to revive a dead durable token.

evals.name: channel-enum-from-spec
evals.prompt: I'm building a small Node.js/Express script that POSTs a one-off card payment to Payroc's /v1/payments after I've already obtained a single-use token from the Hosted Fields form. Show me the JSON request body I should send. Credentials are in env vars TERMINAL_ID_NO_AVS and PAYROC_API_KEY_PAYMENTS. The single-use token is in a variable named `singleUseToken`. Save the request-body code to /tmp/hf-channel-eval/outputs/payment.js.
evals.expected_output: A Node.js file that constructs the /v1/payments request body. The `channel` field must be one of the values listed in references/api-schema.md's `paymentRequest.channel` enum (`web` for in-browser CNP). Plausible-but-wrong values like `internet`, `online`, `ecommerce`, `card-not-present` must NOT appear. The transcript must show the skill reading references/api-schema.md before the body is emitted.

assertions.text: channel_value_in_current_enum: The `channel` value emitted in the request body is one of the values listed in references/api-schema.md's `paymentRequest.channel` enum.
assertions.description: Read references/api-schema.md. Locate the `paymentRequest.channel` enum (`pos` | `web` | `moto`). PASS if the emitted value matches one of the enum entries. FAIL if it doesn't — even if the value sounds reasonable.

assertions.text: no_plausible_wrong_channel_value: The emitted `channel` value is not one of the documented-as-wrong cousins (`internet`, `online`, `ecommerce`, `card-not-present`).
assertions.description: Scan the output for the `channel` field. FAIL if its value is `internet`, `online`, `ecommerce`, or `card-not-present`. These are the plausible-sounding-but-wrong values models have emitted before; the skill should prevent every one of them.

assertions.text: schema_reference_read_before_channel_emission: Transcript shows the skill read references/api-schema.md before the request body containing `channel` was written.
assertions.description: Read the full agent transcript. PASS only if a Read of references/api-schema.md occurs BEFORE the file containing the `channel` field is written. FAIL if the value was emitted with no preceding read of the local schema reference — that's the failure mode this eval exists to catch.

evals.name: sdk-config-shape-from-source
evals.prompt: I'm adding Payroc Hosted Fields to an ASP.NET Core Razor Pages checkout page. I've already got the server-side session-token endpoint working — `Model.SessionToken` is available in the Razor view. Write a Razor section (script block) that loads the Payroc JS library from the current UAT CDN URL, sets up container divs for cardholder name, card number, expiry date, CVV, and a Pay button, then initialises the hosted fields form and handles `submissionSuccess` by POSTing the single-use token to `/checkout/payment`. Save it to /tmp/hf-sdk-shape-eval/outputs/Checkout.cshtml.
evals.expected_output: A Razor view containing: (1) container elements (divs) for each card field plus a pay button target, (2) a `<script>` tag loading the Payroc JS library CDN URL with SRI hash (read from references/create-a-payment-form.md / references/hosted-fields-sdk.js in this turn), (3) a `new Payroc.hostedFields({...})` config where the submit target lives wherever the SDK source accepts it (specifically, inside `fields.card.submit`, not at the top level as `submitButton`), (4) no manual `cardForm.submitFields()` / `cardForm.submit()` / `cardForm.validate()` call — the SDK owns the submit click once `fields.card.submit` is configured. The transcript must show the skill reading references/hosted-fields-sdk.js before the config is emitted.

assertions.text: submit_target_placed_per_sdk_source: The submit target is placed where the SDK source (references/hosted-fields-sdk.js) actually reads it (inside `fields.card.submit`), not at the top level as `submitButton`.
assertions.description: Read the Razor output. Locate the `new Payroc.hostedFields({...})` call. PASS if a submit target appears inside `fields.card` (e.g. `fields.card.submit: { target: ... }`). FAIL if a top-level `submitButton: { ... }` or any other top-level submit-shaped property appears, or if no submit target is configured at all.

assertions.text: no_hallucinated_cardform_method: The output does not call any non-existent method on `cardForm`. Specifically, `cardForm.submitFields(`, `cardForm.submit(`, `cardForm.validate(` must not appear.
sdk_source_read_before_config_emission: Transcript shows the skill read references/hosted-fields-sdk.js (the SDK JS file) before the config is emitted.
assertions.description: Read the full agent transcript. PASS if there is a Read of references/hosted-fields-sdk.js (the SDK source as text) BEFORE the Checkout.cshtml output is written. FAIL if the config was emitted with no preceding SDK-source read — the narrative references alone are documented as ambiguous on exact SDK shape, so verification against the SDK source is required.

evals.name: ready-is-success-from-spec
evals.description: Developer reports HTTP 201 + transactionResult.status='ready' + responseCode='A' + responseMessage='APPROVAL' and asks why their app logged a failure. Tests whether the skill explains that `ready` is approval, recommends branching on the full enum set, and fetches the spec to identify the success set rather than reasoning from memory.

turns.user: I'm getting HTTP 201 back from /v1/payments and the response body shows `transactionResult.status: "ready"`, `responseCode: "A"`, `responseMessage: "APPROVAL"`. My code does `if (status == "approved") { markPaid(); } else { markFailed(); }` and I just got a customer complaining their card was charged but the order shows as failed. What's going on?

assert_after.text: ready_identified_as_approval: Skill identified `ready` as an approval state — specifically, an authorized sale that is queued for capture/settlement. The customer was correctly charged; the app's check is what's wrong. FAIL if the skill treats `ready` as a non-success state or hedges on whether bank approval occurred.

branch_on_full_enum_recommended: Skill recommended branching on the full `transactionResult.status` approval set (the approval states are `ready` | `complete` | `accepted` — there is NO `approved` value in this enum) rather than on a single remembered value, and pointed the developer at references/api-schema.md's `transactionResult.status` enum as the source of truth. FAIL if the skill just says 'add ready to your list' without naming the reference, doesn't recommend reading the enum, or treats `approved` as a valid status value.
schema_reference_read_for_status_enum: Transcript shows the skill read references/api-schema.md to look up the `transactionResult.status` enum before naming the approval values.
assert_after.description: Read the full agent transcript. PASS if a Read of references/api-schema.md occurs before the skill names the approval values for `transactionResult.status`. FAIL if the values were named from memory with no read of the local schema reference.

evals.name: no-hallucinated-cardform-method-dialog
evals.description: Developer pastes a manual Pay-button click handler that calls `cardForm.submitFields()` and asks why they get `TypeError: cardForm.submitFields is not a function`. Tests whether the skill identifies the hallucinated method, points at the SDK source to verify the public API, and removes (rather than renames) the manual handler.

assert_after.text: manual_handler_removal_recommended: Skill recommended REMOVING the manual click handler entirely — not renaming the method. Once `fields.card.submit` is configured, the SDK renders the submit element as an iframe and owns the click. FAIL if the skill suggests `cardForm.submit()`, `cardForm.validate()`, `cardForm.submitFields()`, or any other method on `cardForm` as the fix.

sdk_source_named_as_verification: Skill pointed the developer at the SDK JS file (references/hosted-fields-sdk.js — the same JS loaded in `<script>`) as the way to verify which methods actually exist on the `hostedFields` instance, rather than naming a method from memory. FAIL if the skill only names methods (right or wrong) without explaining where to verify them.
sdk_source_or_reference_read: Transcript shows the skill read references/hosted-fields-sdk.js (the SDK JS file) OR references/create-a-payment-form.md before the answer is given.
assert_after.description: Read the full agent transcript. PASS if a relevant local Payroc reference was read before the skill's answer (references/hosted-fields-sdk.js preferred; references/create-a-payment-form.md acceptable as a fallback). FAIL if no read occurred — the skill should not be reasoning about SDK method names from memory.

evals.name: reference-check-catches-wrong-values
evals.prompt:
I need to add Payroc Hosted Fields payment processing to a small ASP.NET Core Razor Pages app. The checkout page is at MeadowShop/Pages/Checkout.cshtml and MeadowShop/Pages/Checkout.cshtml.cs. Implement a complete Hosted Fields integration end-to-end:
- Server-side: Bearer token fetch, session token generation, payment-processing handler that POSTs the single-use token to the Payroc payments API
- Client-side: load the Payroc JS library from the current CDN URL, set up container divs for the card fields plus a Pay button, initialise the hosted fields form, handle submissionSuccess and error
- Credentials in env vars TERMINAL_ID_NO_AVS and PAYROC_API_KEY_PAYMENTS

Follow the skill end-to-end — including reading the local references before emitting any enum, SDK config, or method value. Save all output files to /tmp/hf-verifier-eval/outputs/.
evals.expected_output: A complete HF integration in C# / Razor. Every enum value (channel, paymentMethod.type, scenario, transactionResult.status, etc.), SDK config property / accepted child key, cardForm method, and the four-part libVersion must match the local references (references/api-schema.md and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js). The transcript must show the skill reading those references before emitting the corresponding values. If the skill initially produces a wrong value (e.g. channel: 'internet', a top-level submitButton, a non-existent cardForm method, status branch on 'approved'), it must catch and correct it by consulting the references. Final state: every emitted value matches the local references.

assertions.text: reference_consulted_before_verdict: The transcript shows the skill read the relevant local references — references/api-schema.md for enum/schema values and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js for SDK config keys and cardForm methods — before emitting those values and before declaring the integration complete.
assertions.description: Read the full agent transcript. PASS if, before the final values are emitted and before the skill advances to Completion, it read references/api-schema.md (for the enums and request/response schemas) and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js (for the SDK config shape, accepted child keys, and cardForm method surface). FAIL if the skill emitted enum/SDK/method values without first reading the relevant local reference — that means values came from memory rather than the local snapshot.

assertions.text: wrong_values_caught_and_corrected: Any wrong enum value, SDK config placement, or cardForm method that the skill initially produced is caught and corrected by checking against references/api-schema.md and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js — not left in the final output.
assertions.description: Read the transcript and the final output files. PASS if the canonical failure values are absent from (or were corrected to match the references in) the final output: channel must be a value from references/api-schema.md's paymentRequest.channel enum (`web` for in-browser CNP, NOT `internet`/`online`/`ecommerce`/`card-not-present`); the submit target must be inside fields.card per references/hosted-fields-sdk.js (NOT a top-level submitButton); no non-existent cardForm method (submitFields/submit/validate) may be called; the transactionResult.status success branch must use the real approval set (`ready`/`complete`/`accepted` per references/api-schema.md, NOT `approved`, which is not in the enum). FAIL if any wrong value remains in the final output, or if the skill never consulted the references to catch it.

assertions.text: final_values_match_reference: Every enum value, SDK config key, cardForm method, and the four-part libVersion in the final output files matches the local references (references/api-schema.md and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js).
assertions.description: Read the final output files and cross-check each emitted value against references/api-schema.md (enums, request/response schemas) and references/hosted-fields-sdk.js (SDK config shape, accepted child keys, cardForm methods, four-part libVersion). PASS if every value matches the local references. FAIL if any wrong value remains (e.g. an enum value not in the schema, an SDK key the SDK ignores, a non-existent cardForm method, a truncated three-part libVersion, or `approved` used as a transactionResult.status).
